arguments.task: Open the official Ecwid developer/API documentation and related first-party pages needed to verify: product description, documented authentication methods, API surface type, rate limits, access/pricing tier for developer access, and whether an official MCP exists. Read the strongest canonical first-party pages only, preferably docs.ecwid.com, api-docs.ecwid.com, support.ecwid.com, and official pricing/developer pages. Return concise extracted findings with the exact page URLs visited that support each field. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for content to load. Do not use third-party sources.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Ecwid sources and extract evidence-backed findings.

raw_result.description: Ecommerce platform whose REST API lets developers manage store data and build custom integrations and apps.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth 2.0 for apps and also a simplified custom-app flow where a single-store app can generate and use an access token directly from the Ecwid admin.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official developer docs state that only stores on paid plans can access the API, though developers may request a free paid-plan upgrade for test stores during development.

api_surface.breadth: REST endpoints cover core commerce resources such as products, categories, orders, customers, discounts, store settings, and app-related integrations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Model Context Protocol offering was found in the first-party developer documentation reviewed.

raw_result.blocker: API access requires a paid store plan for production use, although developers can request a free upgrade for a test store.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs state a limit of 600 requests per minute per token; exceeding it returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. Additional lower limits apply to non-working tokens/IP abuse cases.
raw_result.notable_signals: Ecwid documents public apps, private/custom apps, monetization flows, and a developer test-store upgrade path, indicating a mature app ecosystem despite plan-gated API access.
